description_long: This is a retro-styled, floor-standing control cabinet with a robust, angular body finished in a matte red color. The front panel features two main display areas: a rectangular screen on the left and a large circular display on the right, both surrounded by an array of tactile buttons, switches, and indicator lights. The control surface is slightly slanted for ergonomic access, with metallic and plastic components visible around the controls. The top of the cabinet has two sets of horizontal ventilation slits, suggesting internal electronics that require cooling. The sides and rear are plain, with the rear panel being solid and unadorned, while the base is reinforced with a metallic strip. This type of cabinet is typically used for monitoring or controlling electrical or mechanical systems in urban infrastructure or industrial settings.
description: A robust, retro red control cabinet with dual display panels, tactile buttons, and ventilation slits, designed for floor-standing use in urban or industrial environments.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cabinet's angular profile, slanted control surface, and plain red side panel.
description_view_1: Front view displaying two main screens, multiple buttons, switches, and indicator lights, with a slanted control surface for user interaction.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring the left, highlighting the cabinet's depth and the edge of the control panel.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a solid, unadorned red panel with a metallic base strip and no visible controls or openings.
